programs.name: Medicaid
programs.what_it_does: Provides healthcare coverage through Rhode Island's publicly-funded state Medicaid program for eligible seniors and individuals with disabilities. Covers medical services, long-term care, and related supports administered by the RI Department of Human Services.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors, older adults, and people with disabilities meeting Medicaid eligibility criteria.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Varies by eligibility; covers premiums, deductibles, copays, and long-term services.[2][4]

programs.name: Long Term Services & Supports (LTSS)
programs.what_it_does: Offers customized home and community-based services for older adults and people with disabilities or chronic needs in various settings. Includes waivers and the SSI Enhanced Assisted Living program as part of Rhode Island's administration.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Older adults, individuals with disabilities, or those with chronic care needs eligible through DHS.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Custom services tailored to needs; enhances community living options.[2]

programs.name: PACE (Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers comprehensive medical and social services to eligible frail elderly individuals. Administered through partnerships in Rhode Island focusing on long-term care.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Frail seniors age 55+ who are nursing facility eligible and live in PACE service areas.[4]
programs.benefit_value: All-inclusive care including adult day health, transportation, and home care; value up to full nursing home equivalent.[4]

programs.name: Medicare Savings Programs (MSP): QMB, SLMB, QI
programs.what_it_does: Helps pay Medicare Part A/B premiums, deductibles, copayments, and coinsurance for eligible low-income beneficiaries. In RI, includes Qualified Medicare Beneficiary (QMB), Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary (SLMB), and Qualifying Individual (QI) programs.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Limited income and asset seniors with Medicare; specific limits apply (e.g., income thresholds vary by category).[4]
programs.benefit_value: Full/partial coverage of premiums (e.g., Part B ~$174.70/month in 2024) and cost-sharing.[4]

programs.name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: Federal food benefit program helping low-income Rhode Islanders, including seniors, purchase healthy groceries monthly. Administered through RI DHS.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income older adults and households meeting income eligibility.[5]
programs.benefit_value: Monthly EBT benefits averaging $200-300/person depending on household size/income (2024 est.).[5]

programs.name: Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides energy assistance grants to help with heating costs during winter. RI administers federally funded grants via DHS.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households including seniors; priority for those 60+ or disabled.[6]
programs.benefit_value: One-time grants $200-$1,000+ based on income, household size, and fuel type.[6]

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: Funds home energy efficiency improvements like insulation and sealing to reduce costs. Federally supported and administered through RI agencies.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income homeowners/renters including seniors qualifying via income guidelines.[6]
programs.benefit_value: Free upgrades valued $3,000-$8,000 per home; lifetime energy savings.[6]

programs.name: State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free counseling on Medicare, Medigap, and Part D options. In RI, provided through Office of Healthy Aging and partners.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Rhode Island Medicare beneficiaries seeking insurance guidance.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Free personalized counseling; helps save thousands on premiums/drugs annually.[4]

programs.name: Meals on Wheels of Rhode Island
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to promote health and independence. Partners with RI Community Food Bank and senior centers.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound older adults unable to prepare meals, often low-income.[5]

programs.name: Caregiver Support Programs
programs.what_it_does: Provides respite care, training, and support for family caregivers of seniors. Offered through Office of Healthy Aging and community partners.[1][4]
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of older adults with Alzheimer's, disabilities, or long-term illness.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Respite grants up to $1,600/year; counseling and training services.[1][4]

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers part-time paid community service training leading to employment for low-income seniors. Administered through RI partners like RSVP programs.[6]
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage work ~20 hrs/week; $6,000-$8,000/year plus training.[6]

programs.name: Legal Assistance for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal aid for issues like housing, benefits, and abuse. Through RI Legal Services and Elder Law programs.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ facing civil legal needs.[5]
programs.benefit_value: Pro bono representation; saves $5,000+ in legal fees per case.[5]

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Offers independent advocacy to resolve issues in nursing homes, assisted living, and home care. Focuses on quality improvement and resident rights.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Residents of long-term care facilities and their families in RI.[5]
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy resolving 80%+ of complaints; protects rights statewide.[5]

programs.name: Rhode Island Pharmaceutical Assistance for the Elderly (RIPAE)
programs.what_it_does: RIPAE provides financial assistance to eligible seniors for prescription drug costs not covered by Medicare. It helps pay copays and premiums for Medicare Part D plans, reducing out-of-pocket expenses for medications.[5][7]
programs.who_its_for: Rhode Island residents aged 65+ with limited income and assets, enrolled in Medicare Part D.
programs.whats_unique: State-funded program supplementing federal Medicare Part D specifically for RI seniors, administered through the Office of Healthy Aging.[5][7]
programs.benefit_value: Varies by income; covers copays up to program limits after Medicare payments.

programs.name: At Home Cost Share Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides case management and coordinated care services to help seniors remain safely in their homes. Partners with agencies like Child & Family to connect participants to community resources and long-term supports.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Eligible seniors in Newport County and Providence with limited income, through Rhode Island Office of Healthy Aging.
programs.whats_unique: RI state-funded program offering cost-shared home-based services beyond federal Medicaid waivers, focused on aging in place.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Case management and access to services; cost-sharing based on income.

programs.name: Senior Companion Program
programs.what_it_does: Trains volunteers to provide companionship and support to isolated older adults in homes, adult day centers, and community sites. Helps reduce isolation and assists with daily activities to promote independence.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Isolated seniors and adults with disabilities in Rhode Island, served through Division of Elderly Affairs-funded programs.
programs.whats_unique: State-funded volunteer companion service unique to RI's elderly affairs initiatives, emphasizing community-based support.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Free volunteer companionship hours, tailored to individual needs.

programs.name: RIPTA Senior Reduced Fare Bus Pass
programs.what_it_does: Offers discounted fares on all Rhode Island Public Transit Authority buses for senior riders. Enables affordable access to errands, medical appointments, and recreation statewide.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Rhode Island residents aged 65 and older.
programs.whats_unique: State-specific reduced fare program on RI's public transit system, not a standard federal offering.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Reduced fare (exact discount varies; typically 50% off standard adult fare).
